Pralinaha) and a round is named Vrtta or cylindrical.
[Thus five types of pillars are mentioned here : Rucaka, Vajra,
Dvivajra, Frallnaka and Vrtta. All these are said to be auspicious.
These names are based on the shape of the middle portion. For, the
lower and upper parts will have different motifs.

When the whole pillar is divided into nine equal parts, the
first two will belong to the bottom and the last two to the top. They
are called Vahana or Support, Ghata or Pot, Padma or Lotus, and
Uttaroftha or Upper Up. Designs and artistic motifs can oe carved in
these parts.
[Utpala says : "tJrTtfcst m l" The names
Gha\a and Padma are used here because they are actually shaped

like them. The commentator refers to some other opinion which
makes Uttarotfha, the eighth part and Fadma, the ninth.
